exercises.title: Build a Timer Context Manager
exercises.description: Implement a Timer class with __enter__ and __exit__ that measures how long a code block takes. Use time.time() and print the elapsed time when the block exits.
exercises.starterCode:
import time

class Timer:
    def __init__(self, name=""):
        self.name = name
        self.elapsed = 0.0

    def __enter__(self):
        # TODO: Store start time using time.time()
        # TODO: Return self
        pass

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
        # TODO: Compute elapsed = time.time() - start
        # TODO: Store in self.elapsed
        # TODO: Print: "[Sort] Elapsed: X.XXXs"
        # Return False (don't suppress exceptions)
        pass


# Test with a simple loop (should take near 0 time)
with Timer("Sort") as t:
    data = sorted(range(10000))

# elapsed should be a small positive float
print(t.elapsed >= 0)
print(type(t.elapsed).__name__)

exercises.title: DatabaseTransaction Context Manager
exercises.description: Simulate a database transaction context manager. On normal exit it should 'commit'; on exception it should 'rollback'. Use __enter__/__exit__ and check the exc_type parameter.
exercises.starterCode:
class DatabaseTransaction:
    def __init__(self, db_name):
        self.db_name = db_name
        self.operations = []

    def execute(self, sql):
        self.operations.append(sql)

    def __enter__(self):
        # TODO: Print: "BEGIN TRANSACTION on mydb"
        # TODO: Return self
        pass

    def __exit__(self, exc_type, exc_val, exc_tb):
        # TODO: If exc_type is None (no exception):
        #   Print: "COMMIT: 2 operations"
        # TODO: Else (exception occurred):
        #   Print: "ROLLBACK: discarding 1 operations"
        # Return False (don't suppress the exception)
        pass


# Test 1: successful transaction
with DatabaseTransaction("mydb") as tx:
    tx.execute("INSERT INTO users VALUES ('Alice', 30)")
    tx.execute("UPDATE accounts SET balance = 1000")

print()

# Test 2: failed transaction
try:
    with DatabaseTransaction("mydb") as tx:
        tx.execute("DELETE FROM orders WHERE id = 5")
        raise ValueError("Constraint violation")
except ValueError:
    pass
exercises.expectedOutput:
BEGIN TRANSACTION on mydb
COMMIT: 2 operations

BEGIN TRANSACTION on mydb
ROLLBACK: discarding 1 operations
hints: if exc_type is None: print(f'COMMIT: {len(self.operations)} operations')
return False to let the exception propagate

exercises.title: contextmanager Decorator: Managed Resource
exercises.description: Use the @contextmanager decorator from contextlib to create a log_operation(name) context manager that prints 'Starting X' before the block and 'Done X (Xs)' after. Also capture and print elapsed time.
exercises.starterCode:
import time
from contextlib import contextmanager

@contextmanager
def log_operation(name):
    # TODO: Print: "Starting {name}"
    # TODO: Record start time
    # TODO: yield (code in 'with' block runs here)
    # TODO: After yield: compute elapsed
    # TODO: Print: "Done {name} (0.000s)"
    pass


# Test 1
with log_operation("data processing"):
    total = sum(range(100000))

# Test 2
with log_operation("sorting"):
    data = sorted(range(10000), reverse=True)
exercises.expectedOutput:
Starting data processing
Done data processing (0.000s)
Starting sorting
Done sorting (0.000s)

warmup.title: Quick Recall: with Statement
warmup.description: What does this code print? Trace the __enter__ and __exit__ calls.
